name: British Indian Ocean Territory

colors.symbolism: the white wavy stripes representing the Indian Ocean
the blue wavy stripes representing the Indian Ocean
the palm tree representing the tropical vegetation of the islands
the cross of Saint George and saltire of Saint Patrick in the Union Flag canton
the field of the Union Flag canton

description: Alternating wavy horizontal white and blue stripes with the Union Flag in the upper-hoist canton; the fly bears a green palm tree surmounted by the gold St Edward's Crown.
history: Granted by Queen Elizabeth II on 8 November 1990, the 25th anniversary of the territory's establishment. The British Indian Ocean Territory was created on 8 November 1965 from islands previously administered as part of Mauritius and the Seychelles, and now consists of the Chagos Archipelago. In October 2024, the United Kingdom announced a treaty to transfer sovereignty of the Chagos Archipelago to Mauritius while retaining a 99-year lease on the joint US/UK military base at Diego Garcia.

features.symbolism: alternating wavy white and blue stripes representing the Indian Ocean surrounding the Chagos Archipelago
the Union Flag indicating the territory's status as a British Overseas Territory
the tropical palm trees of the Chagos Archipelago
the British Crown, indicating sovereignty over the territory
